mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during rate increases are positive with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and recession risk, triggering institutional portfolio de-risking that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in more than 60% of triggered observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates overheating demand that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales growth and CPI growth yields a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product growth signals expanding corporate revenue bases and improving profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward equity returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of observations when CPI exceeds the 5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during correction phases, with a hit rate below 45%.
